stories.title: Tia Milloy — Day 37 and the 18-Day Clock

x_posts.label: Post 1-1
x_posts.text:
Day 37 in the transfer portal. No confirmed destination.

July 4 weekend is the first natural window before staffs travel to PGF Nationals on July 19.

FSU, Tennessee, and Texas Tech are still in it. The clock is running.

#CollegeSoftball #TransferPortal #MilloyWatch

x_posts.label: Post 1-2
x_posts.text:
18 days until PGF Nationals splits every recruiting staff's attention.

That is Tia Milloy's window — and theirs.

The program that closes her in the next two weeks gets her full focus. After July 19, the competition for attention goes national.

#CollegeSoftball #TransferPortal

facebook.long_form:
Tia Milloy entered Oklahoma's transfer portal on May 25. Today is July 1 — Day 37 — and the former Sooner utility player still has no confirmed commitment.

This is no longer a story about how long a player can wait. It is a story about timing. July changes the recruiting calendar in ways that matter for the three programs still pursuing her — FSU, Tennessee, and Texas Tech. PGF Nationals opens July 19 at Great Park Irvine, and the coaching staffs from every major program in the country will be in southern California evaluating the 2027 high school class. The concentrated recruiting focus that Milloy has received since late May starts to diffuse after that date.

July 4 weekend is the first logical window for a commitment to emerge in July — a natural pause before the travel season begins in earnest. If she commits this week, the story ends cleanly. If she doesn't, the story grows more complicated and more interesting. Milloy's two years of remaining eligibility and her .353 batting average at Oklahoma give her every reason to wait until she is certain. No one should expect her to rush. But the programs chasing her are running out of calendar.

[Posting Window: 9:00 AM ET]

article.headline: Day 37 and No Deal: Tia Milloy's Portal Watch Enters a Tighter July Window

stories.title: Kylie Doherty Commits to Arkansas — SEC Lands the Portal's Best Bat

x_posts.label: Post 2-1
x_posts.text:
Kylie Doherty is going to Arkansas.

27 home runs. 89 RBIs. Patriot League records in both categories. 2026 Patriot League Player of the Year.

The Razorbacks just added the most productive bat in the transfer portal. #CollegeSoftball #Razorbacks

x_posts.label: Post 2-2
x_posts.text:
Kylie Doherty ranked No. 25 overall transfer and No. 5 outfielder in the 2026 portal.

She hit .404 at Boston University with 27 home runs and 89 RBIs — both Patriot League records.

She is headed to the SEC. Arkansas gets a real bat. #CollegeSoftball #TransferPortal

facebook.long_form:
Kylie Doherty is heading to Arkansas.

The Boston University junior — who set Patriot League records with 27 home runs and 89 RBIs in 2026 and was named Patriot League Player of the Year — has committed to the Razorbacks. Doherty hit .404 this past season and was ranked No. 25 overall among all transfer portal players, No. 5 among outfielders. This is her third time in the portal, and she has landed in the SEC.

For Arkansas, this is a significant addition. Doherty's power numbers at Boston University — a program that competes in the Patriot League, not the SEC or any of the traditional power conferences — translated into production that ranked among the best in the country. What she did in a smaller league, she will now be expected to replicate in one of the toughest conferences in college softball. That is the question every mid-major transfer faces in the SEC. But her raw production numbers suggest she is built for the upgrade. The Razorbacks now have one of the most productive bats in the portal installed in their outfield. The 2027 roster just got significantly more dangerous.

[Posting Window: 11:00 AM ET]

stories.title: Jordy Frahm — Coaching, Pregnant, and Still Making History

x_posts.label: Post 3-1
x_posts.text:
Jordy Frahm pitched the entire 2026 season while pregnant.

21-6. 1.37 ERA. 251 strikeouts. 12 saves — led all of Division I.

She is due in December. She joined the Nebraska coaching staff June 5.

The 2026 season just got more remarkable. #Huskers #CollegeSoftball

x_posts.label: Post 3-2
x_posts.text:
Jordy Frahm won back-to-back NFCA National Player of the Year awards.

She is the first to do it since the award was established in 2016.

She did it while pregnant.

Now she is on Nebraska's coaching staff. #CollegeSoftball #Huskers

facebook.long_form:
When Nebraska's season ended, the full story of what Jordy Frahm accomplished had not been told.

Now it has. Frahm revealed that she competed throughout the entire 2026 season while pregnant, with a baby due in December 2026. She posted a 21-6 record, a 1.37 ERA, 251 strikeouts, and 12 saves — the most by any pitcher in Division I. She won NFCA National Player of the Year for the second consecutive season, making her the first player to win back-to-back awards since the honor was established in 2016. Nebraska went 52-8 and reached the Women's College World Series for the first time in 13 years.

She did all of this while pregnant.

Frahm joined the Nebraska coaching staff on June 5. She is transitioning from the most decorated pitching performance in recent college softball history directly into a coaching career at the program she helped define. The 2026 Nebraska season was already the most remarkable stretch in program history. Understanding what Frahm was carrying through every start, every appearance, every save makes it something else entirely.

[Posting Window: 1:00 PM ET]

article.headline: Jordy Frahm Pitched the Whole 2026 Season While Pregnant. Then She Joined Nebraska's Coaching Staff.

stories.title: FSU 2027 — A Rebuild With Real Teeth

x_posts.label: Post 4
x_posts.text:
FSU lost Isa Torres and Jaysoni Beachum in the portal.

They added Ella Dodge and Nicole Edmiaston — 22 combined home runs. Jazzy Francik — ACC Player of the Year — returns.

The ACC's best pitcher stays. FSU has a plan.

#FSUSoftball #CollegeSoftball

facebook.long_form:
The conversation about Florida State softball in 2026-27 has been dominated by what the Seminoles lost.

Isa Torres — who hit .530 with 78 runs scored in 2026 — transferred to Texas. Jaysoni Beachum — who hit .417 with 16 home runs — transferred to Florida. Two of the most productive bats in the ACC in a single portal cycle. The departures are real, and the coverage of them has been extensive.

What has not received equal attention: FSU added Ella Dodge from Tennessee (.285 batting average, 11 home runs) and Nicole Edmiaston from Stetson (11 home runs). Together, that is 22 home runs entering the 2027 roster via the portal. And Jazzy Francik — who won ACC Player of the Year as a pitcher — returns. So does Ashtyn Danley. The Seminoles did not stand still after Torres and Beachum left. They went and got production. They kept the best pitcher in the conference. A program in genuine freefall does not sign 22 home runs worth of replacements and retain an ACC Player of the Year in the circle. FSU has a plan.

[Posting Window: 3:00 PM ET]

article.headline: FSU 2027: The Seminoles Lost Two Stars — Then Added 22 Home Runs and Kept the ACC's Best Pitcher

stories.title: September 1 Countdown — 61 Days to the 2028 Class

x_posts.label: Post 5
x_posts.text:
61 days until September 1.

No direct contact with current ninth-graders until then.

PGF Nationals (July 19) is the last major evaluation event before the window opens.

Under House Settlement rules, every freshman spot counts. #CollegeSoftball #Recruiting

facebook.long_form:
The transfer portal cycle for 2026 is winding down. The portal class for 2027 is largely set. College softball programs have spent the past six weeks reshaping their rosters for next spring. Now — with July 1 arriving and PGF Nationals three weeks away — the sport pivots forward.

September 1, 2026 is the date every recruiting coordinator in women's college softball has circled on their calendar. That is when the official contact window opens for the Class of 2028 — current ninth-graders who cannot receive direct recruiting contact until that date. Today begins the 61-day countdown.

PGF Nationals, which runs July 19-26 at Great Park Irvine, is the most important evaluation event between now and September 1. Programs that have finalized their 2027 rosters are arriving in Irvine with their attention fully on the 2028 class — who they have scouted, who they want to make first contact with on September 1, and which prospects are worth flying across the country to watch one more time before the window opens. Under the House Settlement's 25-scholarship cap, every freshman spot is now more consequential than it has ever been. Programs cannot afford to miss on a class. September 1 is where the next cycle begins.

[Posting Window: 5:30 PM ET]

article.headline: 61 Days: The September 1 Countdown and What It Means for the 2028 Softball Recruiting Class
